sections.title: Get Accessibility Evaluation Report From Accessibility Experts
sections.description:
It’s time to make your websites, applications, and digital content fully accessible and compliant with industry standards! At Accessibility Innovations, we specialize in creating Accessibility Evaluation Reports that align with key standards such as WCAG, Section 508, and PDF/UA.

Our certified experts combine manual testing with advanced automated tools to conduct in-depth audits of your digital assets. This approach identifies barriers preventing users with disabilities from accessing your content and provides actionable solutions.

With our Accessibility Evaluation Report, you’ll gain insights to improve inclusivity, enhance user experience, and achieve regulatory compliance.

image.alt: VPAT report section showing applicable accessibility standards, terms definitions, and WCAG 2.1 report guidelines for conformance evaluation.

sections.title: Accessibility Evaluation Report from Accessibility Innovations

sections.description:
At Accessibility Innovations, we specialize in delivering comprehensive accessibility audit services designed to empower your organization. Our Accessibility Evaluation Reports provide an in-depth assessment of your digital assets, identifying areas where accessibility gaps may exist. With a focus on actionable outcomes, we offer practical, tailored solutions to address these gaps, helping your organization achieve compliance with essential accessibility standards such as the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2.

By leveraging our detailed audits, you can enhance user experience, foster a culture of inclusivity, and confidently meet your accessibility goals. With Accessibility Innovations as your partner, your organization can ensure that its digital presence is inclusive, compliant, and ready to serve a diverse audience.

sections.title: Step 1: Initial Consultation
sections.description: We begin with a consultation to understand your organization’s goals. Whether you aim to meet WCAG 2.2 standards or improve usability for all, we tailor our approach to align with your unique needs.

sections.title: Step 2: Comprehensive Accessibility Audit
sections.description:
Using manual and automated techniques, we evaluate your digital assets across four core areas:

  • Perceivable: Ensuring all content, including images and visual elements, is accessible.
  • Operable: Verifying that interactive elements like buttons and forms work seamlessly with assistive technologies.
  • Understandable: Checking that navigation and content presentation are intuitive and clear.
  • Robust: Ensuring compatibility with browsers, devices, and assistive tools like screen readers.

sections.title: Step 3: Key Findings and Recommendations
sections.description:
Our Accessibility Evaluation Report includes:

  • Compliance Score: A percentage indicating how well your digital assets align with WCAG 2.2 standards.
  • Key Findings: Identified issues such as improper color contrast or unlabeled form fields.
  • Recommendations: Clear, actionable solutions to address each issue for both technical and non-technical teams.

sections.title: Step 4: Remediation Support
sections.description: We collaborate with your team to implement recommended fixes, offering guidance on design adjustments, code updates, or content improvements.

sections.title: Step 5: Final Review and Testing
sections.description: After remediation, we re-evaluate your assets to ensure full compliance with accessibility standards.

sections.title: Step 6: Executive Summary
sections.description: For high-level stakeholders, we provide a concise Executive Summary highlighting your compliance score, key findings, and remediation efforts.

items.title: Compliance Score
items.description: A percentage reflecting your overall adherence to WCAG 2.2 standards, giving a snapshot of accessibility progress.

items.title: Statistics
items.description: A breakdown of accessibility issues categorized under WCAG’s POUR principles (Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust).

items.title: Test Summary
items.description: A detailed overview of testing methods, audit duration, and key observations.

items.title: Key Findings
items.description: Examples of accessibility issues like missing alt text, improper heading structures, or insufficient color contrast.

items.title: Remediation Recommendations
items.description: Specific, actionable steps to address issues, tailored to technical and non-technical teams.

items.title: WCAG 2.2 Checklist
items.description: A comprehensive checklist of success criteria with pass/fail status, offering a clear roadmap to compliance.

sections.title: ADA/WCAG Compliance Help?
sections.description: Undergoing an audit or preparing a compliance report? Our experts are here to assist you every step of the way. Contact Accessibility Innovations today to achieve full accessibility compliance and improve the user experience for all!

items.title: Detailed Reports

sections.description: With Accessibility Innovations USA, your digital assets will meet accessibility standards while delivering a superior user experience for everyone. Let us help you achieve full compliance and inclusivity today!

items.question: Who needs an accessibility evaluation report and why?
items.answer: Any organization that wants to ensure its website or digital content is accessible to everyone, including people with disabilities, should consider getting an accessibility evaluation report. This includes businesses, educational institutions, non-profits, and government entities aiming to meet legal accessibility requirements, such as WCAG or Section 508, or simply improve the usability of their online platforms. It identifies barriers on your website that may prevent users with disabilities from accessing content, helping you ensure inclusivity and comply with accessibility standards like the ADA.

items.question: What standards do you evaluate against?
items.answer: We primarily evaluate against the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1, which are the most widely recognized global standards for web accessibility. We also assess compliance with other relevant standards such as Section 508 (U.S. government) and AODA (Ontario, Canada), depending on your organization’s needs.

items.question: How is the Accessibility Evaluation Report created?
items.answer: The report is generated through a combination of automated tools and manual testing by accessibility experts. It evaluates your website against standards such as WCAG (Web Content Accessibility Guidelines) and provides actionable recommendations for improvement.

items.question: How long does it take to receive an accessibility evaluation report?
items.answer: The timeline for receiving your report depends on the complexity and size of your website or digital content. Typically, the process takes between 2 to 4 weeks. After assessing your site, we will provide you with a more accurate estimate based on the scope of the evaluation.

items.question: Can the Accessibility Evaluation Report help with ADA compliance?
items.answer: Yes, the report highlights areas where your website may fall short of ADA requirements and provides clear guidance to address these issues, making it a valuable tool for compliance.

items.question: What are the benefits of getting an Accessibility Evaluation Report?
items.answer: The report helps improve user experience, ensures legal compliance, reduces the risk of lawsuits, enhances your brand’s reputation, and broadens your audience by making your website accessible to all users.

items.question: Is the Accessibility Evaluation Report suitable for all types of websites?
items.answer: Absolutely! The report can be tailored for any website, whether it’s a small business site, an e-commerce platform, or a large-scale government or educational portal.
